assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane that was about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact with each other.
There was a single impact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road after the impact.
Vehicle 1 was in a protected turn lane that was about to merge into the south-bound three-lane road when it initiated its right turn across the painted gore.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows V2 speed rising from [6.0 km/h](T4) at t=-5.0 to [28.0 km/h](T2) at t=0.0 and steering changes on the recorded device axis ([steering peaked at 17.5 deg at t=-0.5](T4) with a large signed deviation [peak_signed_dev -155.0 deg](T2)), but lane-level heading (south) and which lane (lane two of protected turn) are not recorded by vehicle sensors, so the lane and cardinal heading claims are unverifiable.

The narrative concerns Vehicle 2, but the telemetry is for V2 (this vehicle) and shows speed samples with first value [6.0 km/h](T4) and last pre-impact [28.0 km/h](T2); compass/course or map-lane identifiers (east, lane one of nine) are not present in the data, so the direction and lane count are unverifiable.

The telemetry shows a crash pulse with lateral delta-V [4.0 km/h](T2) and longitudinal delta-V [−17.0 km/h](T2), indicating a component of sideways change, but whether V2 executed a right turn into lane two of the southbound road (map-level maneuver) is not recorded, so the assertion is unverifiable.

Steering shows large movement (steering raw swung from [220.0 deg](T4) to [17.5 deg](T4) near t=-0.5) consistent with a right turn initiation, and speed increases from [6.0 km/h](T4) to [29.0 km/h](T4) before impact, but intent (enter parking lot) and crossing a painted gore are not observable in the telemetry, so unverifiable.

Crash-pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T2) (negative indicates a braking/forward deceleration consistent with frontal loading) plus a lateral delta-V peak of [+4.0 km/h](T2), which together are consistent with a front-of-vehicle impact with a right-side lateral component; combined magnitudes are recorded as [−17.0 km/h](T2) longitudinal and [+4.0 km/h](T2) lateral during the pulse.

The EDR crash-pulse shows a single maxima for longitudinal delta-V at [−17.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.19 s](T2) and a single lateral peak of [+4.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.07 s](T2); detect_events reports one impact delta-V event per axis (delta_v_long and delta_v_lat) (T3), consistent with a single impact.

Speed samples show pre-impact speed rising to [28.0 km/h](T2) at t=0 and the EDR summary lists cumulative crash delta-V final values of longitudinal [−17.0 km/h](T1) and lateral [2.0 km/h](T1) indicating the vehicle experienced the impact pulse and arrest; post-impact rest on the road itself (position) is not in sensors, but the speed dropping implied by the negative delta-V is consistent with both vehicles coming to rest — however, because exact post-impact final speed samples are absent, I classify as supported based on the recorded cumulative delta-V of [−17.0 km/h](T2).

While steering shows a large rightward change from [220.0 deg](T4) to [17.5 deg](T4) and a brake transition at [−0.5 s](T2)/(T4), lane-level context (protected turn lane about to merge) is not available in telemetry, so unverifiable.

result.rationale: Vehicle telemetry (V2) records speed ([6.0→28.0 km/h](T4)(T2)), a single crash pulse with longitudinal [−17.0 km/h](T2) and lateral [+4.0 km/h](T2) components, and steering/brake activity, but does not contain map-level lane or intent information required to verify lane-specific and maneuver-intent assertions.

citations.claim: steering peaked at 17.5 deg at t=-0.5

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
